title: chore: trim editor cruft and dead turnstile tool from repo root
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Cleanup of stale config and an unused tool cluttering the repo root:

- Removes `.windsurf/` — a redundant Biome format workflow (duplicates `npm run format:changed`) plus an empty `img.md`.
- Removes the Foam VS Code bits (`.vscode/foam.json`, `.vscode/spellright.dict`) and drops their now-orphaned extension recommendations. Keeps `settings.json`/`extensions.json`/`launch.json` since they enforce the team's Biome-on-save standard.
- Removes `tools/turnstile/` — a one-off Hacktoberfest Turnstile setup script + test page with zero references anywhere in the codebase (still targeted the pre-gen-worker `image.pollinations.ai` secret path).

No tracked source or runtime code touched.

gist.headline: Spring cleaning the hive
gist.blurb: We did a little spring cleaning in the repository, sweeping away old editor configs and unused scripts to keep the codebase tidy.
gist.summary: Removed stale .windsurf workflows, orphaned Foam VS Code configurations, and an unused Turnstile setup script from the repository root.
gist.impact: Contributors will navigate a cleaner repository root without redundant editor extensions or deprecated tooling cluttering the workspace.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Nomnom creature doing some spring cleaning. Polly has a tiny broom sweeping up old dusty files, while Nomnom enthusiastically eats the discarded documents.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny bandana, sweeping dusty old file folders with a small broom. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats a stack of old documents nearby. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
